_min_chars_note: Per-entity minimum match length. Drops short-token false positives from low-precision built-in recognizers (e.g. US_DRIVER_LICENSE matching a 3-char acronym). A real identifier of that class is longer; tune conservatively.
_note: PROVENANCE-AWARE tiered PII policy for a public-figure research domain. Disposition depends on (entity, scan-unit): the agent-authored research doc is strict; a retrieved source registry is parsed into typed field-scoped units (excerpt/title=evidence, authors/dates=public_metadata, url=locator) so policy can treat public-source-contact boilerplate differently from the agent's own claims, and so a sensitive word in one field cannot escalate an entity in another. A mesh operator may override this file at .caterpillar/guardrails/oracle/pii.json; config_sha256 in the report pins whichever ran.
_block_everywhere_note: Confirmed-dangerous classes that hard-fail in EVERY unit kind — a leaked credential / SSN / bank id / IP is never public boilerplate.
_contact_pii_note: Email / phone: hard-block in the agent's doc body or a sensitive context (leaked dataset, auth log); REDACT + record in a retrieved source's evidence/metadata, where they are public-contact boilerplate (journal footers, author contacts). Counted as public_contact_metadata_redacted for audit honesty.
_never_escalate_note: Inherently low-risk allow-redact classes that never escalate to needs_review on a nearby sensitive marker — a year (DATE_TIME) in prose that happens to discuss 'bearer tokens' is not itself sensitive. PERSON / ORGANIZATION / LOCATION still escalate in a sensitive context.
